pending.body: `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:87-99`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:9047-9085`, and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:9136-9159` now route required final-review reviewer/arbiter availability probes through the new retry helper, but any probe that exhausts its transient budget is then treated as terminal because `should_retry_stage_failure` suppresses all errors recognized by `is_final_review_retry_exhaustion_error` (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:337-357`). A required reviewer or arbiter that hits repeated transient `check_availability` failures will therefore fail the run before any panel member is invoked, with no outer stage retry. That regresses the prior recovery path for pre-invocation probe flaps and still lets a transient backend blip take down the whole `final_review` stage.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 13 -> Plan and Implement
